title: Citroen Relay Lease - Van Sales
body:
[![Citroen Relay Lease](../images/Screenshot-2-600x333.webp)](https://www.vansales.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/04/Screenshot-2.webp)

Citroen Relay Lease The Citroen Relay lease offers a practical solution for businesses looking to expand their fleet without substantial upfront costs. Whether you’re running a delivery service, managing a…

ng a construction company, or need a reliable commercial vehicle, choosing the right lease agreement can save you thousands of pounds annually. In fact, with various leasing options available – from business contract hire to personal lease agreements – finding the perfect match for your needs has never been easier. However, navigating through different lease terms, understanding mileage limits, and comparing deals can feel overwhelming at first. This comprehensive guide breaks down everything you need to know about Citroen Relay leasing, helping you secure the best possible deal in the UK market.

## Understanding Citroen Relay Lease

When searching for a large commercial vehicle, the Citroen Relay stands out as a versatile option that many UK businesses choose to lease rather than purchase outright. This comprehensive section explores what makes the Relay distinctive, why leasing might be advantageous, and how to decide between leasing and buying.

### What is a Citroen Relay?

The Citroen Relay is a large panel van designed for businesses requiring substantial cargo capacity and practical features. This versatile workhorse offers impressive load volumes of up to 17 cubic meters and can handle payloads reaching 1,870kg 1. Available in multiple configurations to suit various business needs, the Relay comes in four lengths, three heights, and with both diesel and electric powertrain options 2.

Following its 2023 update, the Relay received a modern facelift improving its aerodynamics while introducing enhanced technology features 3. The current model offers impressive specifications including:

-   Engine options: 2.2-liter diesel engines producing either 140 bhp or 180 bhp 3
-   Electric e-Relay variant with 75kWh battery offering up to 154 miles range 4
-   Best-in-class load width and low load sill height for easier loading/unloading 2
-   Advanced technology including a 10-inch touchscreen and wireless phone charging on higher trims 3

### Benefits of Leasing a Citroen Relay

Leasing a Citroen Relay presents several advantages for businesses compared to outright purchase. First, monthly payments are typically lower than financing a purchase, making it more accessible to businesses with limited capital 5. Additionally, leasing provides predictable monthly costs, simplifying budgeting and cash flow management throughout your agreement term 5.

Another significant benefit involves upgraded vehicles—leasing enables businesses to drive newer models every 2-5 years 6. This means accessing the latest technology and safety features without committing to long-term ownership.

Maintenance concerns also diminish with leasing arrangements. Most new Citroen Relay vans come covered by the manufacturer’s warranty throughout the lease period, reducing unexpected repair costs 5. Furthermore, VAT-registered businesses can reclaim VAT paid on initial and monthly rentals, provided the van is exclusively for business use 6.

### Lease vs Purchase: Which is Right for You?

Deciding between leasing and purchasing depends largely on your specific business circumstances. Leasing works particularly well if you prefer driving new vans regularly, have limited initial capital, desire predictable payments, or want to avoid depreciation concerns 5.

Conversely, purchasing might be preferable if you value complete vehicle ownership, intend to keep the van long-term, need unlimited customization options, or have sufficient funds for higher initial costs 5.

From a tax perspective, both options offer advantages. When buying a van outright, you can claim 100% of the cost as capital allowance. Meanwhile, with leasing, the monthly payments can be deducted against profits as business expenses 7. Nevertheless, leasing typically includes mileage restrictions and limits on vehicle modifications that ownership doesn’t impose 6.

Ultimately, small businesses and startups often find leasing more advantageous due to lower initial outlays and regular access to newer vehicles 7.

## Citroen Relay Leasing Options in the UK

Navigating the various Citroen Relay lease arrangements available across the UK requires understanding the specific options tailored to different business and personal needs. Each leasing pathway offers distinct financial advantages depending on your usage requirements and tax situation.

### Business Contract Hire

Business Contract Hire represents the most popular option for companies requiring Citroen Relay vans. This leasing structure allows businesses to claim back VAT on monthly payments while keeping initial costs manageable. Citroen officially offers the Relay Luton from £516 per month plus VAT, the Low-Floor Luton from £563 monthly plus VAT, and the Curtainside from £532 monthly 8.

Most Business Contract Hire agreements require an initial rental equivalent to 6-9 monthly payments, followed by fixed monthly installments over contract periods typically ranging from 24-60 months 8. A standard business lease includes annual mileage allowances of 5,000-10,000 miles, with excess mileage charges applying for additional usage 8. Importantly, at the end of the term, vehicles are simply returned without residual value concerns.

### Personal Contract Hire

Personal Contract Hire functions similarly to business leasing but remains structured specifically for individuals rather than registered companies. Unlike business contracts, personal leases include VAT within the quoted monthly figures. This option serves self-employed professionals and sole traders who prefer keeping business and personal finances separate.

Personal leasing generally offers more flexible initial rental options, with packages available starting from minimal upfront payments 9. Some providers even offer Fuel & Go packages that include insurance, minimizing additional expenses 9.

### Finance Lease Options

Finance Lease presents an alternative that combines leasing benefits with greater flexibility at contract end. Under this arrangement, businesses effectively finance the full value of the Citroen Relay with the option to make a final balloon payment 10.

The key advantage of finance lease involves greater control over end-of-contract options—businesses can either pay the predetermined balloon payment to retain the van or sell it to cover the final amount 11. Monthly payments typically range from £351-£381 depending on model specifications and contract length 10.

### Flexible Lease Terms

Flexibility forms a crucial aspect of Citroen Relay leasing, with numerous customization options available. Contract durations commonly span 24, 36, 48, or 60 months, with shorter terms offering higher monthly payments but faster upgrade opportunities 9. Annual mileage allocations range from 5,000 to 40,000 miles, directly affecting monthly costs 9.

Additional flexibility comes through maintenance packages that can be included within monthly payments, covering servicing and repairs throughout the agreement 9. For businesses with seasonal demands, short-term leasing solutions starting from 12 months provide temporary fleet expansion without long-term commitment 4.

## Finding the Best Citroen Relay Lease Deals

Securing an optimal Citroen Relay lease deal requires thorough market research and an understanding of current pricing trends. The commercial vehicle leasing landscape offers various opportunities for businesses seeking cost-effective transportation solutions.

### Current Market Offers

The marketplace currently offers competitive rates across different Citroen Relay configurations. Basic Relay 35 L3 models start from approximately £324 per month, while the Relay 35 L2 variants begin around £333 monthly 12. For businesses requiring enhanced capacity, the Relay 35 Heavy models range from £413 to £436 per month depending on length specifications 12.

Electric options command premium pricing, with e-Relay 40 L3 models starting from £587 monthly and the e-Relay 40 L4 configuration available from £645 12. Notably, the Enterprise trim level appears consistently across most advertised deals, offering excellent value with features including rear parking sensors and cruise control 4.

### Seasonal Promotions

Throughout the year, leasing companies introduce limited-time offers that can substantially reduce costs. Winter and end-of-quarter periods typically yield more attractive terms as dealers strive to meet sales targets. Several providers offer customization options for initial rental amounts, with choices ranging from 1-12 months upfront payment 13.

Moreover, flexible leasing solutions designed for seasonal business peaks exist, including short-term 12-month options that accommodate temporary fleet expansion needs 4.

### Negotiating Better Terms

Effective negotiation strategies can yield significant savings on Citroen Relay leases. Consequently, understanding provider policies regarding mileage allowances becomes essential, as standard contracts typically offer 5,000-10,000 miles annually 14.

Request comprehensive quotes including all potential fees before commitment. Indeed, some providers offer price match guarantees 9, creating leverage for negotiation. Furthermore, businesses should inquire about bundled service packages that include maintenance coverage, potentially saving thousands over the lease term.

### Hidden Costs to Watch For

Beyond the advertised monthly payment, several additional expenses require consideration. Processing fees often apply, typically around £225 plus VAT 14. Excess mileage charges can accumulate quickly if your usage exceeds contract limits.

Early termination penalties may prove substantial if business circumstances change. Additionally, end-of-lease vehicle condition assessments can result in unexpected charges for excessive wear. Lastly, carefully review any luxury car tax implications that might apply when vehicle specifications exceed the £40,000 threshold 14.

## Citroen Relay Specifications and Features

Understanding the technical specifications of the Citroen Relay helps businesses determine which variant best suits their operational needs when considering lease options. The extensive range of configurations ensures there’s a suitable model for virtually any commercial requirement.

### Available Models and Variants

The Citroen Relay comes in multiple body styles – panel van, window van, and chassis cab 2. For businesses seeking versatility, the panel van offers three lengths and multiple roof heights, whereas the chassis cab provides two length options 2. Since 2023, an electric version, the e-Relay, has been added to the lineup, completing Citroen’s electric van offerings 2. This extensive range provides exceptional flexibility when selecting a lease vehicle tailored to specific business needs.

### Cargo Capacity and Dimensions

Beyond ordinary, the Relay boasts impressive carrying capabilities with load volumes ranging from 8m³ in the smallest L1H1 configuration to a cavernous 17m³ in the L4H3 model 3. The maximum payload varies between 1,125kg and 1,870kg depending on specification 3. Inside, the Relay offers class-leading dimensions with 1.42m between wheel arches and 1.87m between partitions 15. The loading sill height of 535-565mm, which can be further reduced by 70mm with optional pneumatic suspension, makes loading considerably easier 15.

### Engine Performance and Efficiency

Primarily, the Relay offers 2.2-liter BlueHDi diesel engines with three power outputs: 120hp with 310Nm torque, 140hp with 340Nm, and a 180hp option 16. All engines are Euro 6.3 emissions-compliant, with CO2 figures reduced by up to 9g/km compared to predecessors 16. Fuel efficiency reaches approximately 37.7mpg on the 140hp diesel variant 17. Essentially, the e-Relay electric option delivers 120hp equivalent power with a 70kWh battery providing up to 139 miles of range 5.

### Technology and Comfort Features

The Relay’s cabin features an ergonomic design with a driver-focused layout. Standard technology includes a 5-inch touchscreen with DAB radio, Bluetooth connectivity, and USB charging ports 17. Safety systems have been substantially enhanced with features like Active Safety Brake, Smart Beam Headlights, and Intelligent Traction Control 5. Higher specification models include additional elements such as cruise control, air conditioning, and rear parking sensors 7. Together, these features create a comfortable working environment throughout the lease term.

## Comparing Citroen Relay with Competitors

Before selecting a Citroen Relay lease, evaluating how this commercial vehicle measures against its main rivals ensures your business investment aligns with operational requirements. Each competitor brings distinct advantages worth considering alongside your leasing decision.

### Citroen Relay vs Ford Transit

The Citroen Relay offers greater body variety with 4 lengths and 3 roof heights compared to Transit’s 3 lengths and 2 heights 6. Despite Transit’s market dominance, the Relay boasts superior cargo capabilities—its largest L4H3 variant provides 17.0cu/m of load volume, outperforming Transit’s 15.1cu/m 6.

Throughout the range, Relay generally delivers better payload capacity for comparable models. Where Transit excels is powertrain options, offering front, rear, or all-wheel drive configurations 6, whereas Relay remains limited to front-wheel drive with manual transmission only. Ford’s cabin environment, especially after its 2020 facelift, appears more modern with an 8-inch touchscreen standard in higher trims 6.

### Citroen Relay vs Mercedes Sprinter

Although the Sprinter represents a premium alternative, the Relay offers comparable functionality at a more accessible price point. The Sprinter delivers superior power output (110kW versus Relay’s 88kW) 18 alongside greater torque at higher RPM, giving it an edge for businesses prioritizing performance.

Both vans accommodate three passengers 18, albeit with differing comfort levels. Engine differences are noticeable—the Sprinter employs a smaller 1,950cc engine compared to Relay’s 2,179cc unit 18. Certainly, the Mercedes commands higher lease costs, making the Relay more attractive for budget-conscious businesses seeking maximum value.

### Citroen Relay vs Volkswagen Crafter

The Volkswagen Crafter provides exceptional driver comfort and technology—features particularly valuable during lengthy delivery routes. Its steering system enables advanced safety features including adaptive cruise control, lane assist, and park assist 1.

The Relay counters primarily with practicality and cost-effectiveness. It offers impressive storage configurations with 13 different cabin compartments 1. According to comparisons, the Relay sometimes delivers greater payload capacity, reaching up to half a ton more than equivalent Crafter models 19.

Crafter’s build quality reflects VW’s reputation for durability, whereas Citroen focuses on practical design elements like the three-part bumper, which reduces repair costs when damaged 1.

## Tips for Securing Affordable Citroen Relay Lease Terms

Maximizing value on your Citroen Relay lease requires attention to several key factors that directly impact overall costs. These considerations go beyond the advertised monthly payment and can significantly affect your total expenditure throughout the agreement.

### Understanding Mileage Limits

Mileage allowance plays a crucial role in determining lease costs, as it helps protect the vehicle’s residual value. Most UK lease contracts come with a minimum requirement of 5,000 miles per year 20. Exceeding your agreed limit triggers excess mileage charges, clearly stated in your contract 20. Importantly, most finance companies won’t approve more than 50,000 miles annually or 180,000 over the entire agreement 20. Therefore, selecting a realistic figure that aligns with your business needs prevents unexpected charges upon vehicle return.

### Maintenance Packages

Adding maintenance coverage to your Citroen Relay lease offers substantial long-term savings. The Platinum Maintenance package covers routine servicing, wear and tear parts replacement, and MOTs 8. Benefits include access to any Citroen Approved Repairer, predictable expenses, and reduced vehicle downtime 8. Optional features worth considering include replacement tire packages and, for e-Relay models, wallbox charging installations 8. These packages can be incorporated into your monthly payment, simplifying fleet management.

### Initial Rental Considerations

The size of your initial payment directly influences subsequent monthly costs. Larger upfront payments typically result in lower monthly rentals 21. Business leases commonly require initial payments equivalent to 3-12 monthly rentals 22. For instance, a Citroen e-Relay Panel Van L3H2 arrangement might request £4,566 initially, followed by 35 monthly payments of £761 8. Many leasing companies offer flexible options ranging from minimal upfront payments to significant deposits up to 50% of the sale price 10.

### End of Lease Options

As your agreement concludes, you’ll typically face two primary choices. Most commonly, you simply return the vehicle, subject to condition assessment and potential excess mileage charges 23. Alternatively, some agreements offer balloon payment options that allow ownership retention 21. For example, a Business Finance Lease might offer a final balloon payment of £12,450 after 36 months 8. Remember that free UK mainland delivery and collection typically come standard with most agreements 24.

## Frequently Asked Questions

## Frequently Asked Questions

Prospective lessees typically have several queries before finalizing a Citroen Relay lease agreement. This section addresses common questions to help you make informed decisions about your commercial vehicle needs.

### How much cargo space does the Citroen Relay offer?

The Citroen Relay provides exceptional cargo capacity, ranging from 8 cubic meters in smaller configurations to an impressive 17 cubic meters in larger models 9. This versatility makes it one of the most spacious vans currently available in the market, suitable for businesses with varying transportation requirements.

### What is the payload capacity of the Citroen Relay?

Depending on your chosen model, the diesel Citroen Relay can carry up to 1,870kg of payload 9. In contrast, the all-electric ë-Relay variant offers a slightly reduced yet still substantial maximum payload capacity of 1,150kg 9, reflecting the additional weight of its battery system.

### What engine options are available?

The Relay comes with efficient diesel engines plus a fully electric model called the ë-Relay 9. This electric version delivers up to 154 miles of range on a single charge plus offers fast-charging capabilities 9, making it suitable for businesses focusing on sustainability yet requiring practical daily operation.

### Will the leased van be delivered to my location?

Yes, most leasing companies include free UK mainland delivery directly to your door as part of the standard agreement 24. Likewise, at the conclusion of your contract term, the leasing company will typically arrange for the van to be collected from your address 24, eliminating transportation concerns.

### Is a warranty included with my lease?

All new Citroen Relay vehicles come with the UK manufacturer’s warranty 24, providing protection throughout your lease period. This coverage offers valuable peace of mind against unexpected mechanical issues or defects.

### Can I add maintenance packages to my lease?

Absolutely, numerous leasing providers allow you to incorporate a comprehensive service and maintenance plan into your monthly payments 24. This option covers most maintenance requirements throughout your lease term, yet remains optional if you prefer to handle servicing independently 24.

### What happens at the end of my lease term?

Upon lease conclusion, you’ll typically return the vehicle for inspection 22. Some agreements alternatively offer balloon payment options allowing you to retain ownership 22. For standard contract hire arrangements, the finance company remains the registered keeper throughout the agreement 22.

## Conclusion

Choosing a Citroen Relay lease offers businesses significant advantages through flexible payment options, modern vehicle features, and comprehensive support packages. The extensive range of configurations, from standard panel vans to specialized electric models, ensures every business finds its ideal match. Though competitors like Ford Transit and Mercedes Sprinter present compelling alternatives, the Relay stands out with superior cargo capacity and cost-effective operation.

Smart lease negotiations can yield substantial savings, especially when considering factors like mileage limits, maintenance packages, and initial rental amounts. The latest Relay models deliver impressive performance metrics, reaching up to 1,870kg payload capacity while maintaining efficient fuel consumption rates. Business owners particularly value the predictable monthly costs and ability to upgrade vehicles regularly through leasing arrangements.

Ultimately, the Citroen Relay proves an excellent choice for companies seeking reliable commercial transport without significant upfront investment. The combination of practical features, competitive pricing, and flexible leasing terms positions it as a standout option in the UK commercial vehicle market.
